member.name: Mr Connor Rand
member.role: MP for Altrincham and Sale West
member.synopsis: Mr Connor Rand is the Labour MP for Altrincham and Sale West , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: ExcludedUK
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what further her engagement her Department will have with the campaigning group, Excluded UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This government is committed to learning lessons from the Covid-19 schemes. Formal evaluations of the Coronavirus Job Retention Scheme (CJRS) and Self-Employment Income Support Scheme (SEISS) have been published. HM Treasury has participated in Module 9 h...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Small Businesses: Directors
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, in the context of financial support for businesses during the Covid-19 pandemic, what evidence her Department used to classify small limited company directors as a fraud risk.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Decisions on eligibility for COVID-19 financial support schemes were taken by the previous government. The Self-Employment Income Support Scheme (SEISS) supported self-employed individuals whose incomes were adversely affected by COVID-19. The scheme was ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Small Businesses: Plastic Packaging Tax
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment her Department has made of the potential impact of the Plastic Packaging Tax on small and medium sized businesses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Plastic Packaging Tax was introduced in April 2022 under the previous government and provides an incentive for businesses to use recycled plastic in packaging, thereby supporting increased recycling and reducing plastic waste.The tax applies to businesses...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Packaging: Recycling
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, if her Department will consider the potential merits of more closely linking the Extended Producer Responsibility system to business profitability instead of packaging volume.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has no plans to link Extended Producer Responsibility for packaging (pEPR) fees to business profitability. Turnover and packaging weight together determine whether a business has pEPR responsibilities, with a de-minimis threshold to reduce ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Services: ICT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how much funding is being allocated to upgrade legacy IT systems in a) the NHS b) individual integrated care boards and c) local authorities responsible for delivering adult social care services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England invests approximately £1 billion per year centrally to operate, support, and upgrade nationally managed technology systems, including the NHS App and core data services.In addition, over the current Spending Review period, NHS England plans to...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Diagnosis: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if guidance is being updated to mandate the use of technology systems to support the delivery of Jess’s Rule across the NHS.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Jess’s Rule was published in September 2024 and rolled out across England as formal clinical guidance, developed with the Royal College of General Practitioners and NHS England. It supports and strengthens general practitioners’ clinical judgement when a ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Diagnosis: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to rollout of Jess’s Rule across the NHS; and if he will set out how technology is being used to make sure problems in care are being spotted and acted upon immediately.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Jess’s Rule was published in September 2024 and rolled out across England as formal clinical guidance, developed with the Royal College of General Practitioners and NHS England. It supports and strengthens general practitioners’ clinical judgement when a ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Services: Disqualification
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many registered social care provider applications were rejected by the CQC due to issues with policies and procedures (a) in England, (b) by region, and (c) by local authority in each of the la
writtenQuestions.answerText: Care providers entering and exiting is a normal part of a functioning market, and local authorities should have appropriate contingency plans in place depending on the services being provided. Under the Care Act 2014, local authorities also have a tempora...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: Decisions about recognition of foreign judgments are made by the UK’s independent judiciary, with safeguards against recognition and enforcement being available.There are various grounds on which a judge may refuse to recognise or enforce a foreign judgme...

writtenQuestions.heading: Civil Proceedings: Hong Kong
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, if he will review the assumption of judicial independence used to recognize civil judgments from Hong Kong, in light of the National Security Law and other developments.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Decisions about recognition of foreign judgments are made by the UK’s independent judiciary, with safeguards against recognition and enforcement being available.There are various grounds on which a judge may refuse to recognise or enforce a foreign judgme...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Civil Proceedings: China
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what steps he is taking to help prevent the principle of judicial comity from being used by the People’s Republic of China to conduct transnational repression against diaspora groups through UK civil courts.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Decisions about recognition of foreign judgments are made by the UK’s independent judiciary, with safeguards against recognition and enforcement being available.There are various grounds on which a judge may refuse to recognise or enforce a foreign judgme...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Non-surgical Cosmetic Procedures: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to regulate non-surgical aesthetic procedures.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 7 August 2025, the Government announced its plans to introduce measures to improve the safety of the cosmetics sector. This included prioritising the introduction of legal restrictions which will ensure that the highest risk cosmetic procedures are bro...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Administration of Justice: Victims
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what steps he plans to take to help improve victims’ confidence in the justice system.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are putting victims first with a record £550 million investment in specialist support services over the next three years, alongside reforms to ensure our justice system delivers swift, fair justice.We will soon be consulting on a new Victims’ Code whic...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: ADHD: Diagnosis
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to bring down NHS ADHD assessment waiting lists.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has recognised that, nationally, demand for assessments for attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) has grown significantly in recent years and that people are experiencing severe delays accessing such assessments. The Government’s ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Manufacturing Industries: Training
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, if he will meet the plastics manufacturing industry to discuss upskilling and the training of existing employees.
writtenQuestions.answerText: My right hon. Friend the Secretary of State and ministers often meet with stakeholders regarding a range of matters. We are currently focusing our skills plans on the Government Priority sectors aligned to the industrial strategy which focuses on eight pr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Cans and Glass: Recycling

writtenQuestions.answerText: A full impact assessment of the Producer Responsibility Obligations (Packaging and Packaging Waste) Regulations 2024 is available. Impact on wider producers is covered in Section 8 of that impact assessment. Around 70% of producers, including many smaller...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Pharmacy: Crimes against the Person
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether a standalone offence of assaulting a retail worker would apply to workers in community pharmacies.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Through our Crime and Policing Bill, this Government has introduced a new specific standalone offence of assaulting a retail worker to help tackle the epidemic of shop theft and violence towards shop workers that we have seen in recent years and protect t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: GP Surgeries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many NHS GP surgeries have closed in each year since 2010; and how many new NHS GP surgeries have been built in each of those years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department does not hold this information centrally.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Primary Care: Buildings
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what estimate he has made of the number of new or redeveloped primary care facilities required following Lord Darzi’s independent investigation into the NHS.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The primary care estate is in mixed ownership between general practices (GPs), property companies, and integrated care boards (ICBs), and can be funded through a variety of routes. The Care Quality Commission regulates sites that provide National Health S...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many speech and language therapists there were in (a) Trafford and (b) Greater Manchester in each of the last five years.

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
